sections.Description:
Much like theBlobberandSpider, the Plasmodia is a slow-moving spawner Vek that fires aSporeusing artillery range. The Spore destroys itself during the enemy turn to shoot a pushing projectile for 1 damage at a certain direction.
As a rare Vek, Plasmodia cannot spawn on the first island and are limited to only one spawn per mission. Plasmodia also cannot spawn with either Blobbers, Spiders, theBlobber Leader,Spider LeaderorPlasmodia Leader.
sections.Strategy:
Compared to the other two notorious spawner Vek, the Plasmodia is the weakest as its minion does not restrict movement and only targets one tile. Since the Spore's attack queues last in the Attack Order it was summoned in,afterother enemy Vek present, it's also possible for another enemy to move in front of the Spore and block its attack range. Spores that were unable to attack will simply wait until the following turn if allowed to live.
Since Spores only target one tile, they can't be as easily baited into attacking a Mech as with the other spawners. So if it doesn't compromise positioning for next turn, try to place Mechs near the Plasmodia itself; if a Spore targets it the following turn, the Mech can then simply move out of the way, and let the Spore damage the Plasmodia instead.
Nevertheless, it is still important to destroy the Plasmodia as quick as possible so that the spores do not linger.
sections.Trivia: - Before version 1.2.76b, the Plasmodia had no restrictions on spawning outside of the usual caps set for rare Vek, meaning that it could spawn with other spawning Vek and the Spider Leader.

The

is a rare

from
Into The Breach
introduced in the Advanced Edition. They are only present if Advanced Enemies are turned on at the start of a new timeline.
Description
[
]
Much like the
Blobber
and
Spider
, the Plasmodia is a slow-moving spawner Vek that fires a
Spore
using artillery range. The Spore destroys itself during the enemy turn to shoot a pushing projectile for 1 damage at a certain direction.
As a rare Vek, Plasmodia cannot spawn on the first island and are limited to only one spawn per mission. Plasmodia also cannot spawn with either Blobbers, Spiders, the
Blobber Leader
,
Spider Leader
or
Plasmodia Leader
.
Strategy
[
]
Compared to the other two notorious spawner Vek, the Plasmodia is the weakest as its minion does not restrict movement and only targets one tile. Since the Spore's attack queues last in the Attack Order it was summoned in,
after
other enemy Vek present, it's also possible for another enemy to move in front of the Spore and block its attack range. Spores that were unable to attack will simply wait until the following turn if allowed to live.
Since Spores only target one tile, they can't be as easily baited into attacking a Mech as with the other spawners. So if it doesn't compromise positioning for next turn, try to place Mechs near the Plasmodia itself; if a Spore targets it the following turn, the Mech can then simply move out of the way, and let the Spore damage the Plasmodia instead.
Nevertheless, it is still important to destroy the Plasmodia as quick as possible so that the spores do not linger.
Trivia
[
]
Before version 1.2.76b, the Plasmodia had no restrictions on spawning outside of the usual caps set for rare Vek, meaning that it could spawn with other spawning Vek and the Spider Leader.
